Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|12=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|12=draft}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=draft}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=draft}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|pH|ja|水素イオン指数|12=draft}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|12=draft}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|12=draft}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=draft}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=draft}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|pH|ja|水素イオン指数|12=draft}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|12=draft}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|ja|宇都宮線|12=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|12=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|12=draft}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=draft}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=draft}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|pH|ja|水素イオン指数|12=draft}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|12=draft}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|ja|宇都宮線|12=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|12=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

revision.comment: 39 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:塩原元湯温泉 up to rev 100245278
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 塩原元湯温泉 |画像 = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg |コメント = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須塩原市]] |交通アクセス = [[西那須野駅]]より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で約40分で[[塩原温泉駅]]へ。[[タクシー]]などに乗り換え約15分。 |泉質 = [[炭酸水素塩泉]] |泉温 = 50.6 |湧出量 = |pH = 6.5 |液性の分類 = 中性 |浸透圧の分類 = 低張性 |宿泊施設数 = 3 |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ 塩原温泉旅館組合] |特記事項 = }} '''塩原元湯温泉'''（もとゆおんせん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須塩原市]]（旧国[[下野国]]）にある[[塩原温泉郷]]の一つの[[温泉]]。 元湯温泉は塩原温泉駅のある[[古町温泉]]より車で更に約15分ほど奥に入ったところに現在3軒の温泉旅館がある。塩原温泉発祥の地と言われている。 == 泉質 == * 含硫黄 - ナトリウム - 塩化物・炭酸水素塩泉（硫化水素型）低張性中性高温泉 ** [[水素イオン指数|pH]]6.5 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]50.6℃ 当地にある[[旅館]]大出館所有の源泉には、[[黒湯]]としては珍しい[[湯の花]]による黒湯が存在する。 === 効能 === * 胃腸病、心臓病、高血圧症、糖尿病、むちうち症、神経痛、切り傷、やけど、リュウマチ、婦人病、皮膚病、肥満症 ''※注 : 効能はその効果を万人に保証するものではない'' == 温泉街 == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
夕食の一例
]] 塩原温泉郷の奥に3軒（「元泉館」「ゑびすや」「大出館」）の[[温泉宿]]がある。 === 歴史 === 「元湯」の名前が示すように、塩原温泉郷の発祥の湯である[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html 元湯（もとゆ）ってこんな温泉] 那須塩原市。 西暦806年（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年）に如葛仙なる人物が温泉を発見したことにより始まったとする説『温泉 第68号第2巻』日本温泉協会 2000年2月 P10-12のほか、発見者を猟師とする説がある。如葛仙の正体は明らかでなく、民話の伝承者によると[[737年]]（[[天平]]9年）に没したとされ、矛盾が生じる。このため地元の郷土史研究会では、[[矢板市]]長井にある寺山観音寺を[[806年]]に開山した徳一大師が発見者ではないかと推測している{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title= 開湯1200年記念の経緯 - 塩原温泉郷土史研究会|date=20190330043156}}。 かつては元湯千軒（実際は80軒程度）といわれるように多数の温泉宿があったが、[[1659年]]（[[万治]]2年）の地震による[[土石流|山津波]]により埋没し、温泉も湧出しなくなり家主などの住民も各地へ移住した[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html 大出館のご案内] 大出館。後年、塩原元湯温泉神社を再建したところ、温泉も湧出するようになり、前述のように現在は3軒の温泉宿がある。 == 交通アクセス == * [[宇都宮線]][[西那須野駅]]より[[ジェイアールバス関東西那須野支店|JRバス]]で約40分の[[塩原温泉駅|塩原温泉駅（塩原温泉バスターミナル）]]へ。塩原温泉バスターミナルから[[タクシー]]や宿の送迎車（予約制）に乗り換え約15分。 ** 塩原温泉バスターミナルまでは、私鉄線や高速バスなど複数ルートもある。詳しくは[[塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|塩原温泉バスターミナル利用できる路線]]を参照。 * [[東北自動車道]][[西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|西那須野塩原IC]]より[[国道400号]]にて約40分。 == 脚注 == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ 塩原温泉旅館組合] {{温泉}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しおはらもとゆおんせん}} [[Category:塩原温泉郷]] [[Category:那須塩原市の地理]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[塩原元湯温泉]] to [[Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:

夕食の一例

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|pH|ja|水素イオン指数|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|ja|宇都宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|pH|ja|水素イオン指数|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|ja|宇都宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|ja|宇都宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasushiobara City|qq=}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by {{ill|Bus|ja|バス (交通機関)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}} from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Station|qq=}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|qq=}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara City|ja|那須塩原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasushiobara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|qq=}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|qq=}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|pH|ja|水素イオン指数|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pH|qq=}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|ja|宇都宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Line|qq=}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|qq=}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|qq=}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Expressway|qq=}} via {{ill|Route 400|ja|国道400号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 400|qq=}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shiobara Onsen Village]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|qq=}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|qq=}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|qq=}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|qq=}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|qq=}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]]

Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|qq=}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|qq=}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|qq=}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|qq=}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:remove from page of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]]

Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|qq=}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|qq=}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|qq=}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|qq=}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|qq=}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[sn:Shiobara Motoyu Onsen]] [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}}

[[commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|qq=|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|qq=|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|qq=|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|qq=|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|qq=|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|qq=|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}}

]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Yunohana|ja|湯の花|1=Yunohana|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohana|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
