[[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11428621}}

See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428621}}

[[commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428621}}

Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]] | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasushiobara City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], in the historic province of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** [[:en:PH|pH]] 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishi-Nasuno Station]] on the [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 400|Route 400]]. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. [[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|PH|en|PH|WD=Q40936|lt=pH}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 400|en|Japan National Route 400|WD=Q11421457|lt=Route 400}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428621]] Wikidata name: Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Shiobara Onsen Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|PH|en|PH|WD=Q40936|lt=pH}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 400|en|Japan National Route 400|WD=Q11421457|lt=Route 400}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 400|en|Japan National Route 400|WD=Q11421457|lt=Route 400}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City|1=Nasushiobara}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City|1=Nasushiobara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|PH|en|PH|WD=Q40936|lt=pH|1=PH}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line|1=Utsunomiya Line}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 400|en|Japan National Route 400|WD=Q11421457|lt=Route 400|1=Japan National Route 400}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line|1=Utsunomiya Line}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 400|en|Japan National Route 400|WD=Q11421457|lt=Route 400|1=Japan National Route 400}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen]][[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] {{infobox Onsen | Name = Shiobara Motoyu Onsen | image = Motoyu Onsen 04 fx.jpg | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City|1=Nasushiobara}} | Transportation = About 40 minutes by [[:en:Bus|Bus]] from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi. | Spring Type = {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}} | Temperature = 50.6 | Discharge = | pH = 6.5 | Water property = Neutral | Osmotic Pressure = Hypotonic | Number of Inns = 3 | Total Capacity = | Annual Visitors = | Statistics Year = | External Links = [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] | Special Notes = }} '''Shiobara Motoyu Onsen''' (塩原元湯温泉, Motoyu Onsen) is an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasushiobara City|1=Nasushiobara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, in the historic province of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, and is part of the {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Village|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Village|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. Motoyu Onsen is about 15 minutes further by car from {{ill|Furumachi Onsen|ja|古町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumachi Onsen|1=Furumachi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411528}}, near Shiobara Onsen Station, and currently has three ryokan (Japanese inns). It is said to be the original birthplace of Shiobara Onsen. == Spring Quality == * Sulfur-containing sodium chloride bicarbonate spring (Hydrogen sulfide type), hypotonic, neutral, high-temperature spring. ** {{ill|PH|en|PH|WD=Q40936|lt=pH|1=PH}} 6.5 ** Source spring temperature: 50.6℃ The ryokan Oidekan owns a rare black spring source (kuroyu), characterized by black sediment called {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}. === Benefits === * Gastrointestinal diseases, heart diseases, hypertension, diabetes, whiplash injury, neuralgia, cuts, burns, rheumatism, gynecological disorders, skin diseases, obesity. ''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|
An example of dinner served
]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters. The true identity of Jokassen is unclear, and according to folklore, he died in 737 (Tenpyo 9), causing contradictions. Therefore, the local history research association speculates that Priest Tokuichi, who founded Terayama Kannon Temple in Nagai, Yaita City in 806, might be the discoverer.{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/momiji0286/kaitou1200nen.html|title=History of 1200 years since opening - Shiobara Onsen Local History Research Association|date=20190330043156}} Historically, the area was known as "Motoyu Senken" (meaning a thousand houses, though actually about 80 inns), but they were destroyed by a landslide triggered by an earthquake in 1659 (Manji 2), causing residents to relocate.[https://www.ooidekan.com/info.html About Oidekan] Oidekan Later, when the Shiobara Motoyu Onsen Shrine was rebuilt, the hot spring started flowing again, and today there are three inns. == Access == * Approximately 40 minutes by JR Bus from {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishi-Nasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} on the {{ill|Utsunomiya Line|en|Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q1144130|lt=Utsunomiya Line|1=Utsunomiya Line}} to {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|ja|塩原温泉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}}. Then about 15 minutes by taxi or inn shuttle (reservation required). ** There are multiple alternative routes by private railways and highway buses to Shiobara Onsen Bus Terminal. See {{ill|Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|ja|塩原温泉駅#利用できる路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen Station#Available Routes|1=Q11428633|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428633}} for details. * About 40 minutes from the {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nasuno Shiobara IC|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 400|en|Japan National Route 400|WD=Q11421457|lt=Route 400|1=Japan National Route 400}}. == References == {{commonscat|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{reflist}} == External links == * [https://www.siobara.or.jp/ Shiobara Onsen Inn Association] {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|塩原元湯温泉|version=100245278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩原元湯温泉]] [[ja:温泉]] [[ja:黒湯温泉]] [[ja:仙北市]] [[ja:モール泉]] [[ja:北海道]] [[ja:関東地方]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[ja:岩室温泉]] [[ja:にごり湯]] [[ja:黒湯]] {{Commons category|Shiobara-Motoyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11428621}} [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Shiobara Onsenkyō]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

''Note: These effects are not guaranteed for everyone.'' == Onsen District == [[File:Dinner -Motoyu Onsen.JPG|thumb|240px|

]] In the depths of Shiobara Onsen Village, there are three inns: "Gensenkan," "Ebisuya," and "Oidekan." === History === As indicated by its name "Motoyu" (original hot spring), it is considered the origin of Shiobara Onsen Village.[https://www.city.nasushiobara.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/shioharashisho_sangyokankokensetsuka/onsenjoho/1/2267.html Motoyu (元湯), an onsen like this] Nasushiobara City There are theories that the onsen was discovered in 806 (Daido period) by a figure named Jokassen,"Onsen Vol. 68, No. 2" Japan Onsen Association, February 2000, pp. 10-12 while another theory claims it was discovered by hunters.
